content.text:
<timestamp>Tuesday, Jun 2, 2026, 12:55 PM (UTC+10)</timestamp>
<user_query>
can you find the description of the discovery agent  for me pls, what does the concierge and project team see about it
</user_query>
Looking up the exact Discovery Agent description now, then I’ll show what both the Concierge and Project Team contexts expose about it.
I found the core wiring; next I’m checking the exact Concierge role text and the Project Team member-facing descriptions so you get the literal wording each side sees.

You’ve got **two different “discovery” roles** in this repo, and that distinction matters:

- **Concierge Discovery Agent** (`agents/concierge/discovery_agent.py`)  
  - `name`: `Discovery`  
  - `role` (what Concierge router sees):  
    > “Runs the headless repository discovery workflow and returns the link to the generated artifacts. Route here when the user wants to run discovery on a repository.”  
  - Source: `agents/concierge/constants.py` (`DISCOVERY_MEMBER_ROLE`)

- **Project Team Discovery Steward** (`agents/project_team/build.py`)  
  - `name`: `Discovery Steward`  
  - `role` (what Project Team leader sees):  
    > “Runs the headless discovery workflow on a source repository, including prerequisite verification. Delegate here when the issue requires a discovery run.”  
  - Also echoed in Project Team description:  
    > “Discovery Steward runs the headless discovery workflow and reports artifacts.”

Important nuance: the **runtime behavior/instructions are not hardcoded** in these files; both sides pull prompt bodies from Langfuse:
- Concierge discovery prompt name defaults to `ux/discovery` (`DISCOVERY_AGENT_LANGFUSE_PROMPT_NAME`).
- Project Team steward prompt name is `project-team/discovery-steward` (`DISCOVERY_STEWARD_PROMPT_NAME`).

If you want, I can pull the **actual live Langfuse prompt text** each one is currently using (not just these role summaries).

<manually_attached_skills>
The user has manually attached the following skills to their message.
These skills contain specific instructions or workflows that the user wants you to follow for this request.
Only read the files if needed, the full skill content is inlined here.

Skill Name: babysit
Path: /home/vscode/.cursor/skills-cursor/babysit/SKILL.md
SKILL.md content:
# Babysit PR
Your job is to get this PR to a merge-ready state.

Check PR status, comments, and latest CI and resolve any issues until the PR is ready to merge.

1. Merge conflicts: Intelligently resolve any merge conflicts, preserving the intent and correctness of changes on your branch and the base branch. If intents conflict, abort the merge and ask for clarification.
2. Comments: Review active unresolved comments (including Bugbot) and resolve change requests / bug reports where valid. When fetching GitHub comments, filter out resolved threads first. Read only each comment body and the minimum location/URL needed to act on it; do not read the entire JSON output or other unnecessary payload data. Carefully validate issues reported by Bugbot and only take action on those that are valid; explain when you disagree or are unsure.
3. CI: Fix CI issues caused by changes within this PR's scope. Never change CI checks/workflows just to make failures pass, or make unrelated code changes; if that would be required, report back instead. For merge-blocking failures that seem unrelated to this PR, check whether the branch is behind the base branch and merge latest changes, since another PR may have fixed them. Push scoped fixes and re-watch CI until mergeable + green + comments triaged.
</manually_attached_skills>
<timestamp>Tuesday, Jun 2, 2026, 12:59 PM (UTC+10)</timestamp>
<user_query>
commit and push and /babysit 

</user_query>
I’ll stage the current changes, create a focused commit, push your branch, then start the babysit loop by checking PR status/comments/CI and fixing anything valid.

input.command: git diff -- agents/concierge/constants.py agents/concierge/discovery_agent.py agents/project_team/build.py agents/provisioner/bootstrap_service.py app/main.py tests/unit/concierge/test_discovery_agent.py

content.text: I reviewed the diff: it includes both discovery-role wiring and provisioner bootstrap compatibility updates, so I’ll make one commit that captures both and run targeted unit tests before pushing.

input.description: Run targeted unit tests for changed modules
Stage all changes commit and verify status
input.command:
git add agents/concierge/constants.py agents/concierge/discovery_agent.py agents/project_team/build.py agents/provisioner/bootstrap_script.py agents/provisioner/bootstrap_service.py app/main.py tests/unit/concierge/test_discovery_agent.py tests/unit/provisioner/test_bootstrap_service.py && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(discovery): wire repo-read context and preserve bootstrap compatibility

Give Concierge/Project Team discovery members clearer routing intent while wiring read-only GitHub repo tools into the Concierge Discovery agent. Also restore legacy bootstrap input generation so scaffold post-create scripts that still read input.json continue to work.
EOF
)" && git status --short --branch

input.description: Push commit to tracked remote branch

content.text: Commit is pushed. I’m now running the babysit pass: checking PR mergeability, unresolved review comments, and CI checks, then I’ll address anything valid.

input.description: Get PR status for current branch
Fetch PR metadata and check states
List unresolved review threads with comment bodies
input.command: gh api graphql -f query='query { repository(owner:"trilogy-group", name:"ab-agno") { pullRequests(first:20, states:OPEN, headRefName:"coder/issue-158") { nodes { number reviewThreads(first:100) { nodes { isResolved comments(first:10) { nodes { body url path } } } } } } } }'

input.description: Check current PR check run statuses

content.text: No unresolved review threads; CI is still in progress. I’m watching checks to completion and will fix anything that fails.

input.description: Verify PR mergeability after green checks

content.text:
Committed, pushed, and babysat.

- **Commit:** `3cf18e0`  
- **Branch pushed:** `coder/issue-158` → `origin/coder/issue-158`
- **PR:** [#160](https://github.com/trilogy-group/ab-agno/pull/160)

Babysit status now:
- Merge state is **`CLEAN`**
- No unresolved review threads found
- All checks are **green**: `lint`, `type`, `test-unit`, `conventional-pr-title`, `Semgrep`
- Local working tree is clean

This PR is in a merge-ready state from CI/comments perspective.
